bitty documentation

Documentation

data-* attributes

Overview

  • The HTML side of the bitty API consists of these four attributes.
  • Attributes can be added to HTML elements to send and receive signals through JavaScript methods.

Items

data-connect
Description
  • `data-connect` is an optional attribute that can be added to bitty-8-0 tags.
  • If a `data-connect` attribute exists bitty will attempt to load the module defined by the attribute's value.
  • If a bitty-8-0 tag doesn't have a `data-connect` attribute it will attempt to load a module class from `window.BittyClass` as a fallback.
Examples
Load a default class from `window.BittyClass` if no `data-connect` attribute exists on a `bitty-8-0` tag. - [0010-default-bitty-class-on-window]
HTML
<bitty-8-0></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DA553">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.BittyClass = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_DA553");
  }

  signal_DA553(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Load a `window.NamedClass` directly from that page when the value of `data-connect` is `NamedClass` (and not a remote path). - [0020-connect-to-class-on-page]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5EF10"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_5EF10">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5EF10 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_5EF10");
  }

  signal_5EF10(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Connect to the default class from a remote module. - [0030-connection-to-default-module-class]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0010-data-attributes/items/data-connect/tests/0030-connection-to-default-module-class/remote-javascript.js"
></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_15881">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
// this content is in an external file
// TODO: show the external file here.
</script>
Results
bug
Connect to a specific class from a remote module. - [0040-connect-to-specific-remote-class]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0010-data-attributes/items/data-connect/tests/0040-connect-to-specific-remote-class/remote-javascript.js:ExampleClass"
></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CD4FD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
// this content is in an external file
// TODO: show the external file here.
</script>
Results
bug
data-listeners
Description
Examples
Listen for a specific event type. - [0010-listen-for-event]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9CE5F"></bitty-8-0>

<div 
  class="el-9CE5F" 
  data-listeners="bittylocallistenertest"
  data-send="signal_9CE5F"
></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_9CE5F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9CE5F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-9CE5F");
    const testEvent = new TestEventClass9CE5F();
    testSender.dispatchEvent(testEvent);
  }

  signal_9CE5F(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};

// This test uses a custom event instead of
// something like mouseover to facilitate
// automated test output.
class TestEventClass9CE5F extends Event {
  constructor() {
    super("bittylocallistenertest", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
</script>
Results
bug
Only send signals when the specified listener is fired (i.e. don't respond to `click` or `input` unless they are included in the `data-listeners` value). - [0020-only-send-for-specified-events]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC23DC"></bitty-8-0>

<div 
  class="el-C23DC" 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousevent"
  data-send="signal_C23DC"
></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C23DC">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC23DC = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-C23DC").click();
  }

  signal_C23DC(_, el) {
    // set to `bug` here since this
    // signal should not fire.
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
Set listeners globally for a bitty class directly from the bitty-8-0 tag. - [0030-set-listeners-globally]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="bittygloballistenertest"
  data-connect="ClassAC896"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="el-AC896" data-send="signal_AC896"></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_AC896">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassAC896 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-AC896");
    const testEvent = new TestEventClassAC896();
    testSender.dispatchEvent(testEvent);
  }

  signal_AC896(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};

// This test uses a custom event instead of
// something like mouseover to facilitate
// automated test output.
class TestEventClassAC896 extends Event {
  constructor() {
    super("bittygloballistenertest", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
</script>
Results
bug
Setting a global `data-listeners` attribute on a `bitty-8-0` tag removes the default `click` and `input` listeners. - [0035-global-listener-removes-defaults]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousgloballistenertest"
  data-connect="ClassE7E76"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="el-E7E76" data-send="signal_E7E76"></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E7E76">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE7E76 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-E7E76").click();
  }

  signal_E7E76(_, el) {
    // set to `bug` here since this
    // signal should not fire.
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
The default `click` and `input` listeners can be added back in to the `data-listeners` attribute on a `bitty-8-0` element. - [0037-add-default-to-globals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousgloballistenertest click input"
  data-connect="Class22223"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="el-22223" data-send="signal_22223"></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_22223">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class22223 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-22223").click();
  }

  signal_22223(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Setting an empty string for the value of data-listener on an element prevents it from sending signals. - [0040-empty-string-turns-off-listeners]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1AE48"></bitty-8-0>

<div 
  class="el-1AE48" 
  data-listeners=""
  data-send="signal_1AE48"
></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_1AE48">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1AE48 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-1AE48").click();
  }

  signal_1AE48(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
The default `click` and `input` events can be added back in alongside specified listeners. - [0050-include-default-with-custom]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4BCE1"></bitty-8-0>

<div 
  class="el-4BCE1" 
  data-listeners="mouseover bittytest click input"
  data-send="signal_4BCE1"
></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_4BCE1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4BCE1 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-4BCE1").click();
  }

  signal_4BCE1(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • The specific set of custom listeners is defined based on elements that exist with a `data-listeners` attribute when the module loads. That is, if a new element is added after a bitty module has initialized with a new listener type in a `data-listener` attribute value it won't be picked up.
data-receive
Description
Examples
Receive a single signal - [0010-single-data-receive]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class90DD9"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_90DD9">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class90DD9 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_90DD9");
  }

  signal_90DD9(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Receive multiple signals - [0020-receive-multiple-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCBE64"></bitty-8-0>

<div 
  class="test" 
  data-receive="signal_CBE64 signal_CBE64_2 signal_CBE64_3"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCBE64 = class {
  #updates = [];

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger(
      `signal_CBE64 signal_CBE64_2 signal_CBE64_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_CBE64(_, el) {
    this.#updates.push("bug");
    el.innerHTML = this.#updates[0];
  }

  signal_CBE64_2(_, el) {
    this.#updates.push("bug");
    el.innerHTML = this.#updates[1];
  }

  signal_CBE64_3(_, el) {
    if (this.#updates.length === 2) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Multiple elements receive the same signal - [0030-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class327E5"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class327E5 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger(`signal_327E5`);
  }

  signal_327E5(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
The receiver can access the event that triggered the signal - [0040-event-sent-to-receiver]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1DDF3"></bitty-8-0>

<button 
  class="el-1DDF3"
  data-status="ok"
  data-send="signal_1DDF3"
>Test Button</button>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_1DDF3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1DDF3 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector(".el-1DDF3").click();
  }

  signal_1DDF3(ev, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
data-send
Description
Examples
Send a basic signal from a click event - [0010-basic-send]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassABF58"></bitty-8-0>

<button class="el-ABF58" data-send="signal_ABF58">
  Test Button
</button>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ABF58">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassABF58 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector(".el-ABF58").click();
  }

  signal_ABF58(ev, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>

bug

this.api methods

Overview

Items

addElement(id, content)

TODO: Add error case where the incoming thing isn't a valid element.
Description
Examples
Add an HTML Element to `this.api.element(id, subs={})` from a string. - [0010-add-element-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0CF85"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_0CF85">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0CF85 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.addElement("id-0CF85", `<div>ok</div>`);
    this.api.trigger("signal_0CF85");
  }

  signal_0CF85(ev, el) {
    console.log(this.element["id-0CF85"]);
    if (this.element["id-0CF85"] === `<div>ok</div>`) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Add existing element - [0020-add-existing-element]
HTML
JavaScript
<script>
// el = document.createElement("div")
// el.innerHTML = "ok"
</script>
Results
Add from document fragment. - [0030-add-from-fragment]
HTML
JavaScript
<script>

</script>
Results
addHMTL(id, content)
Description
Examples
Add HTML to the `this.api.html(id, subs={})` collection from a string. - [0010-add-html-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class84554"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_84554">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class84554 = class {
  #id = "id-84554";
  #content = `<div>ok</div>`;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addHTML(this.#id, this.#content);
    this.api.trigger("signal_84554");
  }

  signal_84554(ev, el) {
    const html = this.api.html(this.#id);
    el.innerHTML = html.querySelector("div").innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Add HTML to the `this.api.html(id, subs={})` collection from an existing HTML element (the `.outterHTML` is stored as a string). - [0020-add-html-from-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class40BB2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_40BB2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class40BB2 = class {
  #id = "id-40BB2";

  bittyReady() {
    const element = this.generateElement();
    this.api.addHTML(this.#id, element);
    this.api.trigger("signal_40BB2");
  }

  generateElement() {
    const skeleton = document.createElement("template");
    skeleton.innerHTML = "<div>ok</div>";
    const el = skeleton.content.cloneNode(true);
    return el.firstChild;
  }

  signal_40BB2(ev, el) {
    const html = this.api.html(this.#id);
    el.innerHTML = html.querySelector("div").innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Add HTML to the `this.api.html(id, subs={})` collection from an existing HTML Document Fragment (is stored as a string). - [0030-add-html-from-doc-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9067F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_9067F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9067F = class {
  #id = "id-9067F";

  bittyReady() {
    const fragment = this.generateFragment();
    this.api.addHTML(this.#id, fragment);
    this.api.trigger("signal_9067F");
  }

  generateFragment() {
    const skeleton = document.createElement("template");
    skeleton.innerHTML = "<h1>title</h1><div>ok</div>";
    return skeleton.content.cloneNode(true);
  }

  signal_9067F(ev, el) {
    const html = this.api.html(this.#id);
    el.innerHTML = html.querySelector("div").innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
addJSON(id, content)
Description
Examples
Add a string of JSON to the `this.api.json(id, subs={})` collection. - [0010-add-json-from-text]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassADD80"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ADD80">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassADD80 = class {
  #id = "id-ADD80";
  #content = `{ "status": "ok" }`;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addJSON("id-ADD80", `{ "status": "ok"}`);
    this.api.trigger("signal_ADD80");
  }

  signal_ADD80(ev, el) {
    el.innerHTML = this.json["id-ADD80"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Add JSON to the `this.api.json(id, subs={})` collection from a JavaScript object. - [0020-add-json-from-object]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE5DE2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E5DE2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE5DE2 = class {
  #id = "id-E5DE2";
  #content = { status: "ok" };

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addJSON(this.#id, this.#content);
    this.api.trigger("signal_E5DE2");
  }

  signal_E5DE2(ev, el) {
    const json = this.api.json(this.#id);
    el.innerHTML = json.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Add JSON to the `this.api.json(id, subs={})` collection from a JavaScript object. - [0030-add-json-from-json]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC0779"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C0779">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC0779 = class {
  #id = "id-C0779";
  #content = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addJSON(this.#id, this.#content);
    this.api.trigger("signal_C0779");
  }

  signal_C0779(ev, el) {
    const json = this.api.json(this.#id);
    el.innerHTML = json.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
addSVG(id, content)
Description
Examples
Add a new SVG to `this.api.svg(key, subs = {})` - [0010-add-svg]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class507FA"></bitty-8-0>
<div data-receive="display_signal_507FA"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_507FA">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class507FA = class {
  #_id = "id-507FA";

  #_content = `
<svg version="1.1" width="300" height="200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>`;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addSVG(this.#_id, this.#_content);
    this.api.trigger("signal_507FA");
  }

  signal_507FA(_, el) {
    if (this.api.svg(this.#_id).querySelector("text").innerHTML === "ok") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_507FA");
  }

  display_signal_507FA(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.api.svg(this.#_id));
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
addTEXT(id, content)
Description
Adds a new text template to `this.api.text(key, subs={})` with the given key
Examples
Make a text string from a template without substitutions. - [0010-add-text]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassDD649"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DD649">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassDD649 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addTEXT("id-DD649", "ok");
    this.api.trigger("signal_DD649");
  }

  signal_DD649(ev, el) {
    el.innerHTML = this.text["id-DD649"];
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
addStylesheet(styles)
Description
  • Adds a string of text as a new adopted stylesheet.
  • Returns the new adopted stylesheet
Examples
Load a block of CSS as an adopted stylesheet - [0010-add-stylesheet]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF3756"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_F3756">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF3756 = class {
  #newStyles = `:root { --el-F3756: crimson; }`;
  #sheetThatWasAdded;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("run_signal_F3756 verify_signal_F3756");
  }

  run_signal_F3756(_, __) {
    this.#sheetThatWasAdded = this.api.addStylesheet(
      this.#newStyles,
    );
  }

  verify_signal_F3756(_, el) {
    const result = this.#sheetThatWasAdded.rules[0].cssText;
    if (result === this.#newStyles) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
TODO: clearLogs()
Description
Examples
TODO: clearStorage(key|, newValue|) - clear storage and re initialize with a new value if the otional value was passed.
Description
Examples
TODO: collectionLogLevel() - get the current collection log level
Description
Examples
copy(selector)
Description
  • Copies the text from the selected element.
  • If the element has a `.value` (e.g. it's a form element), then that value is copied.
  • TODO:If the element doesn't have a `.value` the `.innerHTML` is copied instead.
  • Copying is done asynchronously.

NOTE: Because of the way copying text interacts with the operating system's copy/pasteboard these tests must be verified manually. Use the `Test Manually` buttons to copy text from the elements. Then, paste it into another document to verify the correct value was copied.

Examples
Copy text from a textarea (requires manually testing) - [0010-copy-from-value]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class8CF2E"></bitty-8-0>
<textarea class="el-8CF2E">text from textarea id-8CF2E</textarea>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_8CF2E">Test Manually</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class8CF2E = class {
  async signal_8CF2E(_, __) {
    await this.api.copy(".el-8CF2E");
  }
};
</script>
Results
Copy text from innerHTML (requires manual testing) - [0020-copy-from-inner-html]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class45310"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-45310">text from div id-45310</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_45310">Test Manually</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class45310 = class {
  async signal_45310(_, __) {
    await this.api.copy(".el-45310");
  }
};
</script>
Results
text from div id-45310
Show a brief `Copied` message on the copy button - [0030-update-copy-button]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5DFF2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-5DFF2">text from div id-5DFF2</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_5DFF2">Test Manually</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5DFF2 = class {
  #copyButtonTimeout;

  async signal_5DFF2(ev, _) {
    await this.api.copy(".el-5DFF2");
    ev.target.innerHTML = "Copied";
    if (this.#copyButtonTimeout !== null) {
      clearTimeout(this.#copyButtonTimeout);
    }
    this.#copyButtonTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
      ev.target.innerHTML = "Test Manually";
    }, 1500);
  }
};
</script>
Results
text from div id-5DFF2
debug(payload)
Description
Create a `debug` level log entry.
Examples
Create a `debug` log message with a text string. - [0010-basic-trace]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE3057"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_E3057">bug</div>

<div>
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. 
Use this button if you want to output an example.
</div>
<button data-send="manual_review_signal_E3057">
  Generate Console Log Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE3057 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_E3057");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_E3057(_, el) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.debug("example-E3057");
    const got = this.api.logs()[0].payload;
    if (got === "example-E3057") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  manual_review_signal_E3057(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("debug");
    this.api.debug("Example log - example-E3057");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. Use this button if you want to output an example.
Create a `debug` log message with an object. - [0020-object-trace]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBE22A"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_BE22A">bug</div>

<div>
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. 
Use this button if you want to output an example.
</div>
<button data-send="manual_review_signal_BE22A">
  Generate Console Log Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBE22A = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_BE22A");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_BE22A(_, el) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.debug({ example: "example-BE22A" });
    const got = this.api.logs()[0].payload.example;
    if (got === "example-BE22A") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  manual_review_signal_BE22A(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("debug");
    this.api.debug({ example: "example-BE22A" });
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. Use this button if you want to output an example.
Customize the console output of `debug` log messages. - [0030-custom-output]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class3248B"></bitty-8-0>
<div>
  Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_3248B">
  Generate Customized Console Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class3248B = class {
  signal_3248B() {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("debug");
    this.api.setOutputLogFunction("debug", (log) => {
      console.log(`${new Date()} - Custom Log Output - see below`);
      console.log({
        log_type: "some_name",
        message: log,
        whatever: ["alfa", "bravo", "charlie"],
      });
    });
    this.api.debug("custom debug output example-3248B");
  }
};
</script>
Results
Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
Customize the collection of `debug` log messages. - [0040-custom-collection]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7A367"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_7A367"></div>

<div>
  Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_7A367">
  Generate Customized Console Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7A367 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    // Disable output to avoid testing noise then
    // fire trigger for automatic test.
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_7A367");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_7A367(_, el) {
    this.api.setCollectionLogFunction("debug", (payload) => {
      return {
        custom_key: "custom_value_7A367",
        payload: payload,
      };
    });
    this.api.debug({ object_example: "test_value_7A367" });
    if (this.api.logs()[0].custom_key === "custom_value_7A367") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  // Provide method to re-enable console logging
  // output and displaying messages when the
  // example button is activated manually.

  signal_7A367(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("debug");
    this.api.debug("text based log from custome example 7A367");
    this.api.debug(
      { exampleLogObject: "7A367", message: "alfa bravo charle" },
    );
  }

  // NOTE: The default log output function expects
  // the content of the log message to be in a
  // `.payload` property on the object passed to
  // it by the collection function. It will output
  // `undefined` to the console if it's not
  // available. If the signature of the object you
  // create with a custom collector function doesn't
  // contain `.payload` you'll likely want to use
  // `this.api.setOutputLogFunction()` to accommodate
  // your object.
};
</script>
Results
Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
TODO: delay(ms) - delay for `ms` and the restart the delay if the same element gets passed the same signal.
Description
TODO: checks to see if a timeout with the current element and method exists. if it does, it clears it and reject the associated promise. Then, it sets a new timeout with the ms and and returns a promise. Assuming it works as expected this means you can keep delaying an action every time you send a signal to an element through the given method.
Examples
error(payload)
Description
Create a `error` level log entry.
Examples

    this.api.error("example-CA4D6");
    const got = this.api.logs()[0].payload;
    if (got === "example-CA4D6") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  manual_review_signal_CA4D6(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("error");
    this.api.error("Example log - example-CA4D6");

};

JavaScript
<script>
window.Class45D2C = class {
  bittyReady() {
    // Disable output to avoid testing noise then
    // fire trigger for automatic test.
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_45D2C");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_45D2C(_, el) {
    this.api.setCollectionLogFunction("error", (payload) => {
      return {
        custom_key: "custom_value_45D2C",
        payload: payload,
      };
    });
    this.api.error({ object_example: "test_value_45D2C" });
    if (this.api.logs()[0].custom_key === "custom_value_45D2C") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  // Provide method to re-enable console logging
  // output and displaying messages when the
  // example button is activated manually.

  signal_45D2C(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("error");
    this.api.error("text based log from custome example 45D2C");

      { exampleLogObject: "45D2C", message: "alfa bravo charle" },

  // it by the collection function. It will output
  // `undefined` to the console if it's not

Description
Examples
TODO: fetchSVG(url|, options={}|)
Description
Examples
TODO: fetchTemplates(url|, options={}|) - Used to pull down an HTML file with script tags in it that has text/plain, text/html, etc... that then get loaded into their corresponding collections.
Description
Examples
fetchTEXT(key, url [, options = {}])
Description
  • Attempts to fetch a text file from the given `url`
  • If the fetch succeeds, the contents are added into the text collection at `this.api.text(key)`
  • The methods returns an object with `{ ok: true|false, error: null|payload}`
  • If the fetch succeeds, the returned value is `{ ok: true, error: null }`
  • If the fetch fails, the returned value is `{ ok: false, error: payload }`, where payload is the log connection format for the `error` level.
Examples
Fetch a file and load it's contents into the `this.api.text(key)` collection. - [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class415EB"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_415EB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class415EB = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_415EB");
  }

  async signal_415EB(ev, el) {
    const key = "key-415EB";
    const url =
      "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0020-api-methods/items/fetchTEXT/tests/0010-basic-fetch/payload.txt";
    await this.api.fetchTEXT(key, url);
    el.innerHTML = this.text[key].trim();
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Pass an object for fetch options. - [0030-basic-options]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE1FBF"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E1FBF">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE1FBF = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_E1FBF");
  }

  async signal_E1FBF(ev, el) {
    const key = "key-E1FBF";
    const url =
      "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0020-api-methods/items/fetchTEXT/tests/0030-basic-options/payload.txt";
    const options = {
      // Options in this object are added
      // directly to the internal fetch call.
      //
      // NOTE: The documentation server doesn't
      // provide the responses necessary to provide
      // a live example here. Check on the `fetchJSON()`
      // entry for a live example.
    };
    const response = await this.api.fetchTEXT(key, url, options);
    if (response.ok === true && response.error === null) {
      el.innerHTML = this.text[key];
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
The response is an error if the fetch failed. - [0040-fetch-error]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class8E9F6"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_8E9F6">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="logcheck8E9F6">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class8E9F6 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_8E9F6");
  }

  async signal_8E9F6(ev, el) {
    console.error(
      "The 404 error for `/intentionally-missing-file-8E9F6.txt` is part of the test suite. It is an expected error.",
    );
    const key = "key-8E9F6";
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file-8E9F6.txt";
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    const response = await this.api.fetchTEXT(key, url);
    if (response.ok === false && response.error.payload.status === 404) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
    this.api.trigger("logcheck8E9F6");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
Fetching errors are added to the `this.api.logs()` array. - [0050-error-logging]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class6D83C"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_6D83C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class6D83C = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_6D83C");
  }

  async signal_6D83C(ev, el) {
    console.error(
      "The 404 error for `/intentionally-missing-file-6D83C.txt` is part of the test suite. It is an expected error.",
    );
    const key = "key-6D83C";
    const url = "/intentionally-missing-file-6D83C.txt";
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    await this.api.fetchTEXT(key, url);
    if (this.api.logs()[0].payload.type === "fetchError") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
TODO: Redoe putting stuff in `this.storage(key)` (origianl notes: loadStorage(key, fallback) - See notes in `saveStorage` about funciontality.) - parse things first to make sure they are valid and then restringify to move into storage. If the value doesn't parse, don't set the key. Do throw an error.
Description
  • this.api.loadStorage(key) expects the payload to be stringified JSON with a single `data` key at the root of the object
  • [] Load key if it exists
  • [] `this.storage[key]` is undefined if localstroage doesn't have key and no fallback is set
  • [] `this.storage[key]` gets a fallback if localstorage doesn't have an existing key, but a fallback is defined
  • [] `this.storage[key]` gets the value from localstorage if it exists even if a fallback was defined
Examples
Load `this.storage[key]` from `key` in `localStorage` if it exists. - [0010-load-storage-if-it-exists]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF598E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F598E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF598E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    localStorage.setItem(
      "example-F598E",
      JSON.stringify({ data: { status: "ok" } }),
    );
    this.api.trigger("signal_F598E");
  }

  signal_F598E(_, el) {
    this.api.loadJSON("example-F598E");
    el.innerHTML = this.api.json("example-F598E").status;
  }
};
</script>
Results

TODO: html(key [, subs = {}])
Description
Examples
renderJSON(key, subs [, pretty])

- TODO: Use `data-key` for the key instead of `id` so it's consistent with the rest of the data-* approach. - TODO: Return undefined and log an error if something breaks with the parsings.

Output an object by defult if you pass a third argument to it with "pretty" it's pretty print as text instead . If you pass "text" it'll be a single line of text without pretty printing.
Description
  • Pulls json data in from script tags with type of `application/json`
  • TODO: Add context on how json is stored as a string to allow text find/replace
  • TODO: Set up what happens if there's data on the page and you call loadJSON(key) with the same key: the load should override if it's there. If not, the value from the page simply continues to exist.
Examples
Load data from a script tag with type of `application/json` and an `id` that's used as the key when calling `this.api.data(key)` - [0010-load-json-from-script-tag]
HTML
<script id="id-7DA62" type="application/json">
  { "status": "ok" }
</script>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7DA62"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_7DA62">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7DA62 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_7DA62");
  }

  signal_7DA62(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = this.api.json("id-7DA62").status;
  }
};
</script>

bug
JSON parsing error - [0020-json-parsing-error]
HTML
JavaScript
<script>

</script>
Results
svg(key [, subs = {}])
Description
SVGs loaded into the page from script tags with type of `image/svg+xml` that have an id attribute that's used for the key.
Examples
Load an svg from the page's scripts with type image/svg+xml - [0010-load-page-svgs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassFCADA"></bitty-8-0>

<script id="id-FCADA" type="image/svg+xml">
<svg version="1.1"
     width="300" height="200"
     xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">ok</text>
</svg>
</script>

<div data-receive="display_signal_FCADA"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_FCADA">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassFCADA = class {
  #_svg;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_FCADA");
  }

  signal_FCADA(_, el) {
    this.#_svg = this.api.svg("id-FCADA");
    el.innerHTML = this.#_svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_FCADA");
  }

  display_signal_FCADA(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.#_svg);
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
SVG with subs `this.api.svg(key, subs={})` - [0020-svg-with-subs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB3CA3"></bitty-8-0>

<script id="id-B3CA3" type="image/svg+xml">
<svg version="1.1" width="300" height="200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">TARGET</text>
</svg>
</script>

<div data-receive="display_signal_B3CA3"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B3CA3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB3CA3 = class {
  #_svg;
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_B3CA3");
  }

  signal_B3CA3(_, el) {
    const subs = { "TARGET": "UPDATED" };
    this.#_svg = this.api.svg("id-B3CA3", subs);
    if (this.#_svg.querySelector("text").innerHTML === "UPDATED") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_B3CA3");
  }

  display_signal_B3CA3(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.#_svg);
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Output an SVG using an array of text strings as a replacement value. - [0030-array-of-text-subs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class88E1C"></bitty-8-0>
<div data-receive="display_signal_88E1C"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_88E1C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class88E1C = class {
  #updated;

  bittyReady() {
    const source = `<svg version="1.1"
     width="300" height="200"
     xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">TARGET-88E1C</text>
</svg>`;
    this.api.addSVG("id-88E1C", source)
    this.api.trigger("signal_88E1C");
  }

  signal_88E1C(_, el) {
    const subs = {
    "TARGET-88E1C": ["o", "k"]
 };
    this.#updated = this.api.svg("id-88E1C", subs); 
    el.innerHTML = this.#updated.querySelector("text").innerHTML;
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_88E1C");
  }

  display_signal_88E1C(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.#updated);
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
HTML Elements don't work as replacements (There might be elements that work. Attempting that is beyond the scope of expectation) - [0040-html-element-as-replace-value]

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB2B5A"></bitty-8-0>
<div data-receive="display_signal_B2B5A"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B2B5A">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB2B5A = class {
  #updated;

  bittyReady() {
    const source = `<svg version="1.1"
     width="300" height="200"
     xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">TARGET_B2B5A</text>
</svg>`;
    this.api.addSVG("id-B2B5A", source)
    this.api.trigger("signal_B2B5A");
  }

  signal_B2B5A(_, el) {
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "example-B2B5A";
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_B2B5A": newEl,
    };
    this.#updated = this.api.svg("id-B2B5A", subs); 
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_B2B5A");
  }

  display_signal_B2B5A(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.#updated);
  }
};
</script>

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassFD9AC"></bitty-8-0>
<div data-receive="display_signal_FD9AC"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_FD9AC">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassFD9AC = class {
  #updated;

  bittyReady() {
    const source = `<svg version="1.1"
     width="300" height="200"
     xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
  <text x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">TARGET_FD9AC</text>
</svg>`;
    this.api.addSVG("id-FD9AC", source)
    this.api.trigger("signal_FD9AC");
  }

  signal_FD9AC(_, el) {
    const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    fragment.innerHTML = "<div>example-FD9AC-1</div><div>example-FD9AC-2</div>";
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_FD9AC": fragment,
    };
    this.#updated = this.api.svg("id-FD9AC", subs); 
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_FD9AC");
  }

  display_signal_FD9AC(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.#updated);
  }
};
</script>

Use an SVG as a replacment - [0080-svg-replacement]

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBED38"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_BED38">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBED38 = class {
  #sourceSVG = `<svg version="1.1" width="300" height="200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
TARGET_BED38
</svg>`;

  #secondSVG = `<svg version="1.1" width="300" height="200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<text class="ClassBED38" x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="blue">ok</text>
</svg>`;

  #updated;

  bittyReady() {
    const source = ``;
    this.api.addSVG("id-BED38", this.#sourceSVG)
    this.api.trigger("signal_BED38");
  }

  signal_BED38(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_BED38": this.#secondSVG,
    };
    this.#updated = this.api.svg("id-BED38", subs); 
    el.innerHTML = this.#updated.querySelector(".ClassBED38").innerHTML;
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_BED38");
  }

  display_signal_BED38(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.#updated);
  }
};
</script>

Use an array of SVGs are replacments - [0090-array-of-svgs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class48111"></bitty-8-0>
<div data-receive="display_signal_48111"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_48111">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class48111 = class {
  #sourceSVG = `<svg version="1.1" width="300" height="200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="green" />
TARGET_48111
</svg>`;

  #secondSVG = `<svg version="1.1" width="300" height="200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="blue" />
</svg>`;

  #thirdSVG =  `<svg version="1.1" width="300" height="200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<text class="Class48111" x="150" y="125" font-size="60" text-anchor="middle" fill="goldenrod">ok</text>
</svg>`;

  #updated;

  bittyReady() {
    const source = ``;
    this.api.addSVG("id-48111", this.#sourceSVG)
    this.api.trigger("signal_48111");
  }

  signal_48111(_, el) {
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_48111": [this.#secondSVG, this.#thirdSVG]
    };
    this.#updated = this.api.svg("id-48111", subs); 
    el.innerHTML = this.#updated.querySelector(".Class48111").innerHTML;
    this.api.trigger("display_signal_48111");
  }

  display_signal_48111(_, el) {
    el.replaceChildren(this.#updated);
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
renderTEXT(key [, subs = {}])
Description
  • Outputs the text from the page's storage as text after doing a find/replace for the subs.
  • The find replace items are set up in a object where they keys are the strings to find and the values are what to replace the found items with
  • Find and replace is global across the content (i.e. every instance of the `find/key` string is replaced with the `replace/value` content)

  • Replacement values can be:

    • a string
    • an array of strings
    • an html element
    • an array of html elements
    • a document fragment
    • an array of document fragments
    • an SVG
    • an array of SVGs

    In the cases of html elements, document fragments, and svgs, the values are turned into their text forms.

Examples
Output text output without substitutions. - [0010-basic-output]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class95A32"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_95A32">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class95A32 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addTEXT("id-95A32", "example-95A32");
    this.api.trigger("signal_95A32");
  }

  signal_95A32(ev, el) {
    const expected = "example-95A32";
    const got = this.api.renderTEXT("id-95A32");
    if (expected === got) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Output text with plain text substitutions. - [0020-with-subs]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4BF6E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_4BF6E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4BF6E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addTEXT(
      "id-4BF6E",
      "example-4BF6E TARGET_4BF6E",
    );
    this.api.trigger("signal_4BF6E");
  }

  signal_4BF6E(ev, el) {
    const subs = { "TARGET_4BF6E": "UPDATED_4BF6E" };
    const expected = "example-4BF6E UPDATED_4BF6E";
    const got = this.api.renderTEXT("id-4BF6E", subs);
    if (expected === got) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Output text with an array of plain text substitutions. - [0030-with-array-of-text-subs]

    const subs = {
      "TARGET_335C4": ["OUT_1_335C4", " OUT_2_335C4", " OUT_3_335C4"],
    };
    const expected = "example-335C4 OUT_1_335C4 OUT_2_335C4 OUT_3_335C4";
    const got = this.api.renderTEXT("id-335C4", subs);
    if (expected === got) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>

    this.api.trigger("signal_B840F");
  }

  signal_B840F(ev, el) {

    const subs = {
      "TARGET_B840F": newEl,
    };
    const expected = "<div>example-B840F</div>";
    const got = this.api.renderTEXT("id-B840F", subs);
    if (expected === got) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};

Use an SVG as the replacement value (it gets turned into it's .outerHTML value) - [0080-an-svg]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class57B24"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_57B24">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class57B24 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addTEXT("id-57B24", "TARGET_57B24");
    this.api.trigger("signal_57B24");
  }

  signal_57B24(ev, el) {
    const svg = document.createElementNS("http://www.w3.org/2000/svg", "svg");
    const subs = {
      "TARGET_57B24": svg,
    };
    const expected = "<svg></svg>";
    const got = this.api.renderTEXT("id-57B24", subs);
    if (expected === got) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>

};
</script>
Results
bug
saveJSON(key) -

TODO: update for new `this.storage(key)` approach - notes: saveStorage(key) - TODO: Change from setStroage(key, data). New approach corresponds with `this.api.loadStorage("key", fallback)` which loads the data into `this.storage[key]`. Then, `this.api.saveStorage("key") saves that storage item. There will also be `this.api.clearStorage("key", INITVALUE)` that removes the existing storage and optionally sets it to a new value if `INITVALUE` is passed.
Description
  • [] Saves the state of a `this.api.json(key)` into localStorage.
  • [] The payload from `this.api.json(key)` is inserted into a JSON object that looks like this: `{ data: PAYLOAD }`
  • [] The value is then stringified before saving it into localStorage.
  • [] The JSON value is parsed before saving to make sure it's valid.
  • [] If the value is valid, `this.api.saveJSON(key)` returns `{ ok: true }`.
  • [] If the value is invalid, an error is added to `this.api.logs()` and the returned value from the method is `{ error: DETAILS }`
Examples
Returns true if the JSON was saved successfully. TODO - add test with error if it fails and returns false. - [0010-set-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class73693"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_73693">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class73693 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.addJSON("key-73693", { status: "ok" });
    this.api.trigger("signal_73693");
  }

  signal_73693(_, el) {
    if (this.api.saveJSON("key-73693").ok === true) {
      const data = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("key-73693")).data;
      el.innerHTML = data.status;
    }
  }

  /*
  #storageKey = "key-73693";

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.setStorage(
      this.#storageKey,
      { status: "ok" },
    );
    this.api.trigger("signal_73693");
  }

  signal_73693(_, el) {
    const data = this.api.getStorageOr(
      this.#storageKey,
      { status: "bug" },
    );
    el.innerHTML = data.status;
  }
  */
};
</script>
Results
bug
Description
Examples
send(payload, signals)
Description
  • Used to send payloads to trigger signals.
  • The payload must be an object.
  • TODO: Document how `type` and `target.dataset.send` are added to the payload object.
  • TODO: Log an error if the payload is not an object.
Examples
Send a single signal with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` to a single receiver. - [0010-send-a-basic-signal]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassABC35"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ABC35">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassABC35 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_ABC35");
  }

  signal_ABC35(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Send a single signal with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` to multiple receivers. - [0015-send-signal-with-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7E257"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_7E257">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_7E257">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_7E257">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7E257 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_7E257");
  }

  signal_7E257(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send multiple signals to a single receiver with `this.api.set(payload, signals)` - [0020-send-multiple-signals-to-single-receiver]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE22AA"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E22AA signal_E22AA_2 signal_E22AA_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE22AA = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.send(
      { status: "ok" },
      `signal_E22AA signal_E22AA_2 signal_E22AA_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_E22AA(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = `alfa-${payload.status}`;
  }

  signal_E22AA_2(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = `${el.innerHTML}-bravo-${payload.status}`;
  }

  signal_E22AA_3(payload, el) {
    if (payload.status === "ok" && el.innerHTML === "alfa-ok-bravo-ok") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Send multiple signals to multiple receivers with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` - [0025-multiple-signals-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC9113"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C9113">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C9113_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C9113_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC9113 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.send(
      { status: "ok" },
      `signal_C9113 signal_C9113_2 signal_C9113_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_C9113(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }

  signal_C9113_2(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }

  signal_C9113_3(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send a signal to a method that doesn't have a corresponding receiver with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` - [0030-send-signals-to-method-without-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5591E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_5591E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5591E = class {
  #_elIsNull = false;
  #_status;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.send({ status: "ok" }, "test_signal_5591E");
  }

  test_signal_5591E(payload, x) {
    this.#_status = payload.status;
    if (x === null) {
      this.#_elIsNull = true;
    }
    this.api.trigger("verify_signal_5591E");
  }

  verify_signal_5591E(_, el) {
    if (this.#_elIsNull === true) {
      el.innerHTML = this.#_status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Signals sent with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` in one bit are received in others. - [0040-signals-sent-from-one-bit-are-received-in-others]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class31A28"></bitty-8-0>
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class31A28_2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_31A28_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class31A28 = class {
  async bittyReady() {
    // delay to let other bit initialize
    await this.api.sleep(100);
    this.api.send({ status: "ok" }, "signal_31A28_2");
  }
};

window.Class31A28_2 = class {
  signal_31A28_2(payload, el) {
    el.innerHTML = payload.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Await signals inside a string of `this.api.send(payload, signals)` (e.g. `this.api.send({status: "ok", "}, "await:SIG1 SIG2")`) - [0070-await-individual-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class8FAC5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_8FAC5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class8FAC5 = class {
  #t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    this.api.send(
      { status: "ok" },
      "await:test_signal_8FAC5 verify_signal_8FAC5",
    );
  }

  async test_signal_8FAC5(payload, el) {
    await this.api.sleep(50);
  }

  verify_signal_8FAC5(payload, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    if ((t2 - this.#t1) > 40) {
      el.innerHTML = payload.status;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Adding an `await` to `this.api.send()` has no practical effect. The api method fires an event that can be picked up by any number of signal methods inside any number of instances from any number of bits. No attempt is made to try to coordinate async/await across all the potential variations. - [0071-awaiting-send-does-nothing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class754F8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_754F8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class754F8 = class {
  #_updated = false;
  #_t1;

  async bittyReady() {
    this.#_t1 = performance.now();
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  THIS DOES NOT WORK AS YOU MIGHT EXPECT  //
    //  Read the description above for why.     //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    await this.api.send({ updated: true }, "await:test_signal_754F8");
    this.api.send({}, "verify_signal_754F8");
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  See the prior example that combines     //
    //  multiple signals inside a single        //
    //  send to accommodate async/await.        //
    //  For example:                            //
    //  this.api.send({}, "await:SIG_1 SIG_2"); //
    //                                          //
    //  See also the next example which         //
    //  demonstrates using `.send() calls       //
    //  inside individual events to utilize     //
    //  async/await across methods.             //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
  }

  async test_signal_754F8(payload, el) {
    await this.api.sleep(50);
    this.#_updated = payload.updated;
  }

  verify_signal_754F8(payload, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  This result is less than the above      //
    //  sleep time because async/await has no   //
    //  practical effect on                     //
    //  this.api.send(PAYLOAD, SIGNALS)`        //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    if (this.#_updated === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Create an async/await chain of signal methods with `this.api.send(payload, signals)` - [0072-async-await-chain]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class03E7C"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_03E7C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class03E7C = class {
  #_t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#_t1 = performance.now();
    this.api.send({ status: "ok" }, "run_signal_03E7C");
  }

  async run_signal_03E7C(payload, _) {
    if (payload.status === "ok") {
      await this.api.sleep(25);
      this.api.send(payload, "run_signal_03E7C_2");
    }
  }

  async run_signal_03E7C_2(payload, _) {
    if (payload.status === "ok") {
      await this.api.sleep(25);
      this.api.send(payload, "verify_signal_03E7C");
    }
  }

  verify_signal_03E7C(payload, el) {
    if (payload.status === "ok") {
      const t2 = performance.now();
      const time2 = t2 - this.#_t1;
      if (time2 > 40) {
        el.innerHTML = "ok";
      }
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
setCSSProperty(key, value)
Description
Examples
Set a CSS custom property variable. - [0010-set-css-property]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCC556"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_CC556">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCC556 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("run_signal_CC556 verify_signal_CC556");
  }

  run_signal_CC556(_, el) {
    this.api.setCSSProperty("--el-CC556", "crimson");
  }

  verify_signal_CC556(_, el) {
    const gotValue = document
      .documentElement
      .style
      .getPropertyValue("--el-CC556");
    if (gotValue === "crimson") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results

    this.api.trigger("signal_916F1");
  }

  signal_916F1(_, el) {
    this.api.setCollectionLogLevel("trace");

JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7CEE5 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_7CEE5");
  }

  async signal_7CEE5(_, el) {
    const t0 = performance.now();
    await this.api.sleep(50);
    const t1 = performance.now();

trace(payload)
Description
Create a `trace` level log entry.
Examples
Create a `trace` log message with a text string. - [0010-basic-trace]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class14556"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_14556">bug</div>

<div>
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. 
Use this button if you want to output an example.
</div>
<button data-send="manual_review_signal_14556">
  Generate Console Log Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class14556 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_14556");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_14556(_, el) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.trace("example-14556");
    const got = this.api.logs()[0].payload;
    if (got === "example-14556") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  manual_review_signal_14556(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("trace");
    this.api.trace("Example log - example-14556");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. Use this button if you want to output an example.
Create a `trace` log message with an object. - [0020-object-trace]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class45C38"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_45C38">bug</div>

<div>
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. 
Use this button if you want to output an example.
</div>
<button data-send="manual_review_signal_45C38">
  Generate Console Log Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class45C38 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_45C38");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_45C38(_, el) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.trace({ example: "example-45C38" });
    const got = this.api.logs()[0].payload.example;
    if (got === "example-45C38") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  manual_review_signal_45C38(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("trace");
    this.api.trace({ example: "example-45C38" });
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. Use this button if you want to output an example.
Customize the console output of `trace` log messages. - [0030-custom-output]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9FCB5"></bitty-8-0>
<div>
  Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_9FCB5">
  Generate Customized Console Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9FCB5 = class {
  signal_9FCB5() {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("trace");
    this.api.setOutputLogFunction("trace", (log) => {
      console.log(`${new Date()} - Custom Log Output - see below`);
      console.log({
        log_type: "some_name",
        message: log,
        whatever: ["alfa", "bravo", "charlie"],
      });
    });
    this.api.trace("custom trace output example-9FCB5");
  }
};
</script>
Results
Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
Customize the collection of `trace` log messages. - [0040-custom-collection]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class13EEA"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_13EEA"></div>

<div>
  Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_13EEA">
  Generate Customized Console Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class13EEA = class {
  bittyReady() {
    // Disable output to avoid testing noise then
    // fire trigger for automatic test.
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_13EEA");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_13EEA(_, el) {
    this.api.setCollectionLogFunction("trace", (payload) => {
      return {
        custom_key: "custom_value_13EEA",
        payload: payload,
      };
    });
    this.api.trace({ object_example: "test_value_13EEA" });
    if (this.api.logs()[0].custom_key === "custom_value_13EEA") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  // Provide method to re-enable console logging
  // output and displaying messages when the
  // example button is activated manually.

  signal_13EEA(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("trace");
    this.api.trace("text based log from custome example 13EEA");
    this.api.trace(
      { exampleLogObject: "13EEA", message: "alfa bravo charle" },
    );
  }

  // NOTE: The default log output function expects
  // the content of the log message to be in a
  // `.payload` property on the object passed to
  // it by the collection function. It will output
  // `undefined` to the console if it's not
  // available. If the signature of the object you
  // create with a custom collector function doesn't
  // contain `.payload` you'll likely want to use
  // `this.api.setOutputLogFunction()` to accommodate
  // your object.
};
</script>
Results
Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
trigger(signals)
Description
  • Used to trigger one or more signals without sending an event or payload to the method.
Examples
Trigger a single signal with a single receiving element. - [0010-trigger-a-single-signal]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class3E263"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_3E263">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_3E263");
  }

  signal_3E263(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>

Trigger a single signal with multiple receivers. - [0015-send-signal-to-multiple-receivers]

<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1B2A9"></bitty-8-0>

<script>

  }

bug
bug
bug
Trigger multiple signals with a single receiver. - [0020-multiple-signals-single-reciever]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class91BD2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_91BD2 signal_91BD2_2 signal_91BD2_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class91BD2 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger(`
      signal_91BD2 
      signal_91BD2_2
      signal_91BD2_3
    `);
  }

  signal_91BD2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "alfa";
  }

  signal_91BD2_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = `${el.innerHTML}-bravo`;
  }

  signal_91BD2_3(_, el) {
    if (el.innerHTML === `alfa-bravo`) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Trigger multiple signals with multiple receivers. - [0025-multiple-signal-trigger]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class77F36"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_77F36">bug</div>

<script>
window.Class77F36 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger(
      `signal_77F36 signal_77F36_2 signal_77F36_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_77F36(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }

  }

  signal_77F36_3(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};

Results
bug
bug
bug
Trigger a single signal that doesn't have any corresponding receivers (i.e. elements with `data-receive="SIGNAL"` attributes that match that match a signal in `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)`). The signal method is triggered once. The second argument passed to it is `null`. - [0030-send-to-method-without-a-receiver]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class63A99"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_63A99">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class63A99 = class {
  #gotSignal = false;
  #gotNull = false;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("test_signal_63A99");
  }

  test_signal_63A99(_, x) {
    this.#gotSignal = true;
    if (x === null) {
      this.#gotNull = true;
    }
    this.api.trigger("verify_signal_63A99");
  }

  verify_signal_63A99(_, el) {
    if (this.#gotSignal === true && this.#gotNull === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Triggered signals from one bit are received in other bits. - [0040-trigger-other-bits]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class38367"></bitty-8-0>
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class38367_2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_38367_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class38367 = class {
  async bittyReady() {
    // delay to let other bit initialize
    await this.api.sleep(100);
    this.api.trigger("signal_38367_2");
  }
};

window.Class38367_2 = class {
  signal_38367_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Triggering an async signal with await followed by additional signals in the same `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)` call waits for the first method to finish before calling the following methods. - [0070-await-async-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0BB6A"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_0BB6A">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0BB6A = class {
  #t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    this.api.trigger("await:test_signal_0BB6A verify_signal_0BB6A");
  }

  async test_signal_0BB6A(_, el) {
    await this.api.sleep(50);
  }

  verify_signal_0BB6A(_, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    if ((t2 - this.#t1) > 40) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
  • Awaiting `.trigger()` (i.e. `await this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)`) has no practical effect. The `.trigger()` method creates and fires an event which is processed independently by a separate internal bitty method (and, each bit on the page processes the events independently). No attempt is made to communicate async/await status across method or bit boundaries.
  • Ideally a warning would be provided if you try to call `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)` with `await`. The current approaches for determining if a method was called with `await` from inside the method itself are both unreliable and computationally expensive. Unfortunately, that makes delivering a warning infeasible.
      • - [0071-awaiting-triggers-does-nothing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB0FE1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_B0FE1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB0FE1 = class {
  #t1;

  async bittyReady() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  THIS DOES NOT WORK AS YOU MIGHT EXPECT  //
    //  Read the description above for why.     //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    await this.api.trigger("await:test_signal_B0FE1");
    this.api.trigger("verify_signal_B0FE1");
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  See the prior example that combines     //
    //  multiple signals inside a single        //
    //  trigger to accommodate async/await.     //
    //  For example:                            //
    //  this.api.trigger("await:SIG_1 SIG_2");  //
    //                                          //
    //  See also the next example which         //
    //  demonstrates using `.trigger() calls    //
    //  inside individual events to utilize     //
    //  async/await across methods.             //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
  }

  async test_signal_B0FE1(_, el) {
    await this.api.sleep(50);
  }

  verify_signal_B0FE1(_, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    //  This result is less than the above      //
    //  sleep time because async/await has no   //
    //  practical effect on                     //
    //  this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)`              //
    //////////////////////////////////////////////
    if ((t2 - this.#t1) < 40) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Create a chain of async/await calls through individual signal methods. - [0072-async-trigger-chain]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class839AC"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_839AC">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class839AC = class {
  #t1;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#t1 = performance.now();
    this.api.trigger("run_signal_839AC");
  }

  async run_signal_839AC(_, __) {
    await this.api.sleep(25);
    this.api.trigger("run_signal_839AC_2");
  }

  async run_signal_839AC_2(_, __) {
    await this.api.sleep(25);
    this.api.trigger("verify_signal_839AC");
  }

  verify_signal_839AC(_, el) {
    const t2 = performance.now();
    const time2 = t2 - this.#t1;
    if (time2 > 40) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
The first argument sent to a signal method from `this.api.trigger(SIGNALS)` is an instance of the `BittyTriggerEvent` class. It contains only a `type: "bittyapitrigger"` and a `target: { dataset: { send: "SIGNALS" } }` designed to mimic the positions of keys in a standard event. - [0075-bitty-trigger-instance]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB68D3"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_B68D3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB68D3 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_B68D3");
  }

  signal_B68D3(ev, el) {
    if (
      ev.type === "bittytriggerapi" &&
      ev.target.dataset.send === "signal_B68D3"
    ) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Triggers can fire signals that don't have a corresponding element with a `data-receive="SIGNAL"` attribute. When this happens the signal method is called once with `null` for the second argument. - [0080-trigger-signal-without-receiver]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7ABBB"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_7ABBB">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7ABBB = class {
  #gotNull = false;

  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("run_signal_7ABBB");
  }

  run_signal_7ABBB(_, x) {
    if (x === null) {
      this.#gotNull = true;
    }
    this.api.trigger("verify_signal_7ABBB");
  }

  verify_signal_7ABBB(_, el) {
    if (this.#gotNull === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
updateText(key, content)
Description
Examples
warn(payload) - TODO: Update output to use `console.warn()`
Description
Create a `warn` level log entry.
Examples
Create a `warn` log message with a text string. - [0010-basic-trace]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassAC409"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_AC409">bug</div>

<div>
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. 
Use this button if you want to output an example.
</div>
<button data-send="manual_review_signal_AC409">
  Generate Console Log Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassAC409 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_AC409");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_AC409(_, el) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.warn("example-AC409");
    const got = this.api.logs()[0].payload;
    if (got === "example-AC409") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  manual_review_signal_AC409(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("warn");
    this.api.warn("Example log - example-AC409");
  }
};
</script>
Results

Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. Use this button if you want to output an example.
Create a `warn` log message with an object. - [0020-object-trace]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA6709"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_A6709">bug</div>

<div>
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. 
Use this button if you want to output an example.
</div>
<button data-send="manual_review_signal_A6709">
  Generate Console Log Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA6709 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_A6709");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_A6709(_, el) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.warn({ example: "example-A6709" });
    const got = this.api.logs()[0].payload.example;
    if (got === "example-A6709") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  manual_review_signal_A6709(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("warn");
    this.api.warn({ example: "example-A6709" });
  }
};
</script>

bug
Console output for is turned off for the automatic test. Use this button if you want to output an example.
Customize the console output of `warn` log messages. - [0030-custom-output]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassDC1DE"></bitty-8-0>
<div>
  Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_DC1DE">
  Generate Customized Console Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassDC1DE = class {
  signal_DC1DE() {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("warn");
    this.api.setOutputLogFunction("warn", (log) => {
      console.log(`${new Date()} - Custom Log Output - see below`);
      console.log({
        log_type: "some_name",
        message: log,
        whatever: ["alfa", "bravo", "charlie"],
      });
    });
    this.api.warn("custom warn output example-DC1DE");
  }
};
</script>
Results
Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
Customize the collection of `warn` log messages. - [0040-custom-collection]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassEE2A5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="automatic_test_signal_EE2A5"></div>

<div>
  Use this button to generate a log message in the console.
</div>
<button class="manual-test" data-send="signal_EE2A5">
  Generate Customized Console Message
</button>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassEE2A5 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    // Disable output to avoid testing noise then
    // fire trigger for automatic test.
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("none");
    this.api.trigger("automatic_test_signal_EE2A5");
  }

  automatic_test_signal_EE2A5(_, el) {
    this.api.setCollectionLogFunction("warn", (payload) => {
      return {
        custom_key: "custom_value_EE2A5",
        payload: payload,
      };
    });
    this.api.warn({ object_example: "test_value_EE2A5" });
    if (this.api.logs()[0].custom_key === "custom_value_EE2A5") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  // Provide method to re-enable console logging
  // output and displaying messages when the
  // example button is activated manually.

  signal_EE2A5(_, __) {
    this.api.setOutputLogLevel("warn");
    this.api.warn("text based log from custome example EE2A5");
    this.api.warn(
      { exampleLogObject: "EE2A5", message: "alfa bravo charle" },
    );
  }

  // NOTE: The default log output function expects
  // the content of the log message to be in a
  // `.payload` property on the object passed to
  // it by the collection function. It will output
  // `undefined` to the console if it's not
  // available. If the signature of the object you
  // create with a custom collector function doesn't
  // contain `.payload` you'll likely want to use
  // `this.api.setOutputLogFunction()` to accommodate
  // your object.
};
</script>
Results
Use this button to generate a log message in the console.

miscellaneous

Overview

Items

bittyReady()
Description
Examples
Use bittyReady() synchronously - [0010-sync-test]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class86B74"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_86B74">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class86B74 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.api.trigger("signal_86B74");
  }

  signal_86B74(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use bittyReady() async - [0020-async-test]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassDDC6C"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DDC6C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassDDC6C = class {
  #delta;

  async bittyReady() {
    const t0 = performance.now();
    await this.api.sleep(50);
    const t1 = performance.now();
    this.#delta = t1 - t0;
    this.api.trigger("signal_DDC6C");
  }

  signal_DDC6C(_, el) {
    if (this.#delta > 45) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bittyReady() only fires once when the module is loaded - [0030-bitty-ready-only-fires-once]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF5BBF"></bitty-8-0>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_F5BBF">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF5BBF = class {
  #count = 0;

  bittyReady() {
    this.#count += 1;
    this.api.trigger("signal_F5BBF");
  }

  signal_F5BBF(_, el) {
    if (this.#count === 1) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
